message:
fix(h2): correct CONTINUATION frame ordering and reject zero WINDOW_UPDATE

Two HTTP/2 protocol conformance fixes:

1. CONTINUATION frame ordering: When a HEADERS frame exceeds
   max_frame_size and must be split, CONTINUATION chunks were queued
   with push_front() which reversed their order, corrupting HPACK
   decoder state on the peer. Switched to push_back() to preserve
   the original byte order per RFC 9113 §4.3.

2. Zero-increment WINDOW_UPDATE: RFC 9113 §6.9.1 requires that a
   WINDOW_UPDATE with an increment of 0 be treated as a PROTOCOL_ERROR.
   Previously this was silently accepted as a no-op. Now returns an
   H2Error immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
